system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "system";
    object      controlDict;
}

application     pimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         0.3;

deltaT          0.0001;

writeControl    runTime;

writeInterval   0.01;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         Euler;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(p)         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,k)      Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,epsilon) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,omega)  Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div(phi,nuTilda) Gauss limitedLinear 1;
    div((nuEff*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.01;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
    }

    pFinal
    {
        $p;
        relTol          0;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|k|epsilon|nuTilda)Final"
    {
        $U;
        relTol          0;
    }
}

PIMPLE
{
    nOuterCorrectors    1;
    nCorrectors         2;
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pRefCell            0;
    pRefValue           0;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   0.001;

vertices
(

blocks
(

);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 1 9 8)
        );
    }
    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (5 13 14 6)
            (6 14 15 7)
        );
    }
    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (1 4 12 9)
            (4 7 15 12)
        );
    }
    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 8 11 3)
            (3 11 10 2)
            (2 10 13 5)
        );
    }
    frontAndBack
    {
        type empty;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 4 1)
            (2 5 6 3)
            (3 6 7 4)
            (8 9 12 11)
            (10 11 14 13)
            (11 12 15 14)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='physicalProperties', folder_name='constant', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    location    "constant";

}

transportModel  Newtonian;

nu              1e-04;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='nut', folder_name='0', content='FoamFile

boundaryField

        value           uniform 0;
    }

    outlet

        value           uniform 0;
    }

    upperWall

        type            nutkWallFunction;

<error_logs>[{'file': 'log.pimpleFoam', 'error_content': 'ERROR: 
cannot find file "/pscratch/sd/p/peijingx/troubleshoot/benchmark_grouping_C_Cmodels/runs/pitzDaily/3/0/omega"

    From function virtual Foam::autoPtr<Foam::ISstream> Foam::fileOperations::uncollatedFileOperation::readStream(Foam::regIOobject&, const Foam::fileName&, const Foam::word&, bool) const
    in file global/fileOperations/uncollatedFileOperation/uncollatedFileOperation.C at line 539.

FOAM exiting'}]</error_logs>
<reviewer_analysis># Error Diagnosis and Resolution

## Error Analysis

The error is clear:

```
cannot find file ".../runs/pitzDaily/3/0/omega"
```

The solver is looking for a file named `omega` in the `0/` directory, but it doesn't exist. This file is referenced in the `fvSchemes` file through the entry `div(phi,omega)`. 

Looking at the history, the solver was previously crashing due to using an incompatible turbulence model (`kEpsilon` vs `kOmegaSST`). We fixed that by changing `momentumTransport` to use `kOmegaSST`. Now the solver has progressed past that stage and is actively solving the `omega` equation, but the `0/omega` file is missing.

Since we are now using `kOmegaSST`, the `omega` field is indeed required by the turbulence model. The `0/omega` file needs to be created.

## Proposed Fix

**Create the `0/omega` file** with appropriate settings for the `kOmegaSST` turbulence model. Based on the reference pitzDaily tutorial and the existing boundary conditions, the `omega` file should look something like:

```
FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       volScalarField;
    object      omega;
}

dimensions      [0 0 -1 0 0 0 0];

internalField   uniform 5;

bounds
{
    min 0;
    max 100;
}

boundaryField
{
    inlet
    {
        type            fixedValue;
        value           uniform 5;
    }

    outlet
    {
        type            zeroGradient;
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type            omegaWallFunction;
        value           uniform 5;
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type            omegaWallFunction;
        value           uniform 5;
    }

    frontAndBack
    {
        type            empty;
    }
}
```

Key points:
- **Dimensions**: `omega` has dimensions `[0 0 -1 0 0 0 0]` (1/time).
- **Internal field**: Start with `uniform 5` (typical for kOmegaSST, related to the square root of turbulent kinetic energy over length scale).
- **Boundary conditions**: Match the wall functions used for `k` and `epsilon` — `omegaWallFunction` on walls, `fixedValue` at inlet, `zeroGradient` at outlet.
- **Bounds**: Reasonable limits to prevent divergence.

This file is essential because the `kOmegaSST` turbulence model solves a transport equation for `omega`, and without it, the solver cannot proceed.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': '0/omega', 'changes': 'Create new file with FoamFile header for volScalarField object omega; Set dimensions to [0 0 -1 0 0 0 0]; Set internalField to uniform 5; Add bounds section with min 0 and max 100; Set inlet boundary to fixedValue with value uniform 5; Set outlet boundary to zeroGradient; Set upperWall boundary to omegaWallFunction with value uniform 5; Set lowerWall boundary to omegaWallFunction with value uniform 5; Set frontAndBack boundary to empty'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>do a Reynolds-Averaged Simulation (RAS) pitzdaily simulation. Use PIMPLE algorithm. The domain is a 2D millimeter-scale channel geometry. Boundary conditions specify a fixed velocity of 15m/s at the inlet (left),zero gradient pressure at the outlet (right), and no-slip conditions for walls. Use timestep of 0.0001 and output every 0.01. Finaltime is 0.3. use nu value of 1e-04.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
